dry_bulk.confluence_note: While spot indices and ROC60 show powerful expansionary momentum, qualitative fundamentals remain stubbornly negative due to record-high Chinese iron ore inventories and contracting steel production.
dry_bulk.summary: The BDI and Capesize indices are at 5-year percentiles above 99%, driven by short-term spot volatility and high oil prices affecting bunker costs. However, persistent negative fundamental signals from China suggest this rally lacks a structural demand-side foundation.
dry_bulk.outlook: Cautionary expansion; technical momentum is strong but fundamentally fragile.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese steel production levels and iron ore portside inventory destocking.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme Z-scores in both clean and dirty segments align with positive fundamentals driven by geopolitical disruptions and unnavigable straits.
tanker.summary: Clean tankers are at a 100th percentile 5-year peak with a staggering 150.1% 60-day ROC, fueled by Middle East conflict and inventory draws. Though recent momentum has turned negative as the market digests gains, the structural supply-demand imbalance remains bullish. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2163.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2907.0 (ROC60 +79.6%).
key_signals: Global Crude On Water down 1.6% to 10.1% intermittently
Middle East-Asia VLCC rates up 71.1% YOY
tanker.outlook: Strong expansionary regime with high volatility expected as geopolitical risks persist.
tanker.watch: Navigability of the Straits and OECD crude oil stock draw rates.

macro_note: The shipping market is bifurcated by geopolitical risk, where tankers benefit from supply-chain dislocation while dry bulk faces headwinds from a slowing Chinese industrial sector despite current high freight rates.
